qa_pairs.question: Where can users find the status of national resources and services?
qa_pairs.answer: Users can find the status on the Status website at [https://status.alliancecan.ca/].

qa_pairs.question: How are updates handled for the national resources status page?
qa_pairs.answer: The national resources status page is updated manually, meaning unplanned events appear only when a staff member has taken note.

qa_pairs.question: What should users consult to find the status of legacy systems?
qa_pairs.answer: Users should consult the appropriate consortium site for the statuses of legacy systems.

qa_pairs.question: Can you provide examples of consortium sites where legacy system statuses can be checked?
qa_pairs.answer: Examples include SciNet, SHARCNET, Centre for Advanced Computing, and ACENET.

qa_pairs.question: Where can one check the status for SciNet legacy systems?
qa_pairs.answer: The status for SciNet legacy systems can be checked at [https://docs.scinet.utoronto.ca].

qa_pairs.question: How do users find the status of SHARCNET systems?
qa_pairs.answer: Users can find the status of SHARCNET systems by visiting [https://www.sharcnet.ca/my/systems].
